label: Hari Seldon
description: The father of Psychohistory and founder of the two Foundations. He spent his life on Trantor developing the mathematical laws that predict the future of human society.

label: R. Daneel Olivaw
description: An ancient humaniform robot who has protected humanity for nearly 20,000 years. He operates under the Zeroth Law: A robot may not harm humanity, or, by inaction, allow humanity to come to harm.

label: Elijah Baley
description: A Plainclothesman from Earth who partnered with R. Daneel Olivaw to solve critical murders on Spacer worlds, eventually helping humanity spread to the stars once more.

label: The Mule
description: A genetic mutant with the mental power to adjust the emotions of others. He nearly destroyed the Seldon Plan by conquering the Foundation before his eventual defeat.

label: Salvor Hardin
description: The first Mayor of Terminus. Known for his quote: 'Violence is the last refuge of the incompetent.' He navigated the first two Seldon Crises through diplomacy and political maneuvering.

label: Trantor
description: The heart of the Galactic Empire. An ecumenopolis covered by metal domes, housing 40 billion people until its Great Sack and eventual decay into a farming world.

label: Terminus
description: A metal-poor planet at the edge of the galaxy. Home to the First Foundation and its Encyclopedia Galactica project, designed to preserve human knowledge.

label: Prime Radiant
description: A device that stores and displays the complex mathematical equations of Psychohistory. It is the primary tool used by the Second Foundation to track the Seldon Plan.

label: Psychohistory
description: A branch of mathematics that deals with the reactions of human conglomerates to fixed social and economic stimuli. It can only predict the behavior of large populations.

label: Gaia
description: A planet where every living and non-living thing shares a common consciousness. It is the ultimate expression of unity and the alternative to Galaxia.

label: Aurora
description: The first Spacer world settled from Earth. It was once the center of robot technology and the home of Dr. Han Fastolfe.

label: The Second Foundation
description: A hidden group of mentalists established by Hari Seldon. Located 'at the other end of the galaxy' at Star's End, they protect the Seldon Plan from unforeseen deviations.

label: Galactic Empire
description: The massive political entity that ruled the entire Milky Way for over 12,000 years before its inevitable collapse predicted by Seldon.

label: Hober Mallow
description: A master trader who became the first 'Merchant Prince' of the Foundation. He replaced religious influence with economic power as the Foundation's primary weapon.

label: Bayta Darell
description: The woman who stopped the Mule. Her realization of the Mule's identity and her quick actions saved the Second Foundation from being discovered.

label: Visi-Sonor
description: A complex multi-sensory instrument that stimulates the optic and auditory nerves directly. It was used by the Mule to manipulate emotions under the guise of music.

label: Solaria
description: The last of the fifty Spacer worlds. Its inhabitants lived in extreme isolation, served by thousands of robots, and eventually evolved into hermaphroditic mentalists.

label: Gladia Delmarre
description: A Solarian woman who traveled to Aurora and eventually accompanied Elijah Baley and Daneel. She played a key role in the final days of the Spacer worlds.

label: Golan Trevize
description: A Councilman of the Foundation who possessed the uncanny ability to reach the right conclusion from incomplete data. He was chosen to decide the future of the galaxy.

label: Janov Pelorat
description: A scholar who spent his life studying the origins of humanity. He accompanied Trevize on his journey to find Earth and the secret behind Gaia.

label: Gaal Dornick
description: A young mathematician from Synnax who joined Hari Seldon on Trantor and witnessed the trial that led to the creation of the First Foundation.

label: Earth
description: The long-forgotten cradle of mankind. By the time of the Foundation, it was a radioactive wasteland, its location lost to history until rediscovered by Trevize and Pelorat.

label: Preem Palver
description: The 19th First Speaker of the Second Foundation. He operated under the guise of a simple farmer to successfully mislead the First Foundation and defeat the Mule's remnants.

label: Arcadia Darell
description: The granddaughter of Bayta Darell. Her adventure as a stowaway helped resolve the mystery of the Second Foundation's location, though she was unknowingly manipulated.

label: Bel Riose
description: The last great general of the Galactic Empire. He led a massive campaign against the Foundation, but his success led to his execution by a paranoid Emperor.

label: Cleon I
description: The last emperor of the Entun dynasty to rule over a relatively peaceful galaxy. He was the patron of Hari Seldon during the early years of Psychohistory.

label: Eto Demerzel
description: The enigmatic First Minister to Emperor Cleon I. In reality, he is R. Daneel Olivaw, using his position to help Seldon establish the Foundation.

label: Dors Venabili
description: A historian and wife of Hari Seldon. She was secretly a humaniform robot built by Daneel to protect Seldon while he developed Psychohistory.

label: Kalgan
description: A planet that was once a resort world for the Empire. It became the capital of the Mule's temporary Union of Worlds.

label: Encyclopedia Galactica
description: A comprehensive collection of all human knowledge. The First Foundation was established under the pretense of creating this monumental work.

label: Siwenna
description: A former provincial capital at the edge of the Empire. It served as the staging ground for Bel Riose's attack on the Foundation.

label: Anacreon
description: One of the first systems to break away from the Empire. It attempted to conquer Terminus during the first Seldon Crisis but was thwarted by Salvor Hardin.

label: Smyrno
description: A rival to Anacreon among the Four Kingdoms. Like its neighbors, it eventually fell under the religious and economic influence of the Foundation.

label: Psychoprobe
description: A device used by Imperial authorities to extract memories and suppress unwanted mental patterns. It represents the coercive power of the declining Empire.

label: Wanda Seldon
description: The granddaughter of Hari Seldon who discovered her own mentalic abilities. She became the core of the group that would form the Second Foundation.
